zh.lifeSummary: 當Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain於大約1145年出生在威爾士時，他的父親Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain37歲，母親Dyddgu ferch Madog26歲。他和Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他於1211年登記服兵役。他於1170年在英國威爾斯被列為baron of cedewen。他去世於1244年在英國威爾斯卡迪甘史爾育斯特拉-福盧享年100歲，埋葬在威爾士卡迪甘史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain于大约1145年出生在威尔士时，他的父亲Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain37岁，母亲Dyddgu ferch Madog26岁。他和Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。他于1211年登记服兵役。他于1170年在英国威尔斯被列为baron of cedewen。他去世于1244年在英国威尔斯卡迪甘史爾育斯特拉-福盧享年100岁，埋葬在威尔士卡迪甘史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rabbitt
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name Rabbode, Radboda (ancient Germanic Radbodo, from elements meaning ‘counsel’ + ‘messenger’).
English: from the personal name Robet or Robot, diminutives of Rob (see Robert ).
English: nickname from late Middle English rabet ‘young rabbit’. This word is not recorded until c. 1398, the usual Middle English word for ‘rabbit’ being cony (see Coney ).

variants.name: McRobbie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): patronymic meaning ‘son of Robbie’, from the personal name Robbie, a Scottish pet form of Robert , with the Gaelic prefix mac ‘son of’.

variants.name: Rabjohn
meanings: English (Devon, Essex): from the Middle English personal name Robechon (of Norman French origin: Old Picard Robichon, Old French Robeçun), a double diminutive of Rob, a pet form of Robert , altered by folk etymology to Robjohn and Rabjohn.

variants.name: Nabb
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived by a nab, Middle English nabbe ‘hillock, knoll’ (Old Norse nabbi ‘projecting peak, hill’), or a habitational name from any of the many minor places in northern and eastern England named with this word, for example Whalley Nab in Blackburn (Lancashire), Nab Scar in Rydal (Westmorland), and The Nab in Burgh Saint Margaret (Norfolk).
English (Lancashire): alternatively, it may be from the Middle English personal names Nabb and Napp, which are probably parallels to Nobb and Nopp, pet forms of Robert . Compare Nobbs .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Aba(dh) ‘son of the abbot’, see McNab .

variants.name: Probert
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Roppert ‘son of Robert’(see Robert ).

variants.name: Rabb
meanings: Scottish and English (Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Rab(b), a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel. Compare Robb .
Hungarian: variant of   Rab  .

variants.name: Rapson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rab(b) (a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel) + -son, with devoicing of /b/ to /p/. Compare Robson and Rabb .

variants.name: McCobb
meanings: Irish: from Mac Hob, ‘son of Hob ‘, an English pet form of Robert . Compare Hobson .

variants.name: McRoberts
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Roibeirt, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of the personal name Robert , with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain was born about 1145, in Wales, his father, Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain, was 37 and his mother, Dyddgu ferch Madog, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. He registered for military service in 1211. In 1170, at the age of 26, his occupation is listed as baron of cedewen in Wales, United Kingdom. He died in 1244, in Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 100, and was buried in Cardiganshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain est né environ 1145, à Galles, son père, Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Dyddgu ferch Madog, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1211. En 1170, à l’âge de 26 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est baron of cedewen à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1244, à Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 100 ans, et a été enterré à Cardiganshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain um 1145 in Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain 37 und seine Mutter Dyddgu ferch Madog 26. Er hatte mit Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1211 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1170, als er 26 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als baron of cedewen in Wales, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb 1244 im Alter von 100 Jahren in Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Cardiganshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain è nato il circa 1145 nel Galles, il suo padre, Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Dyddgu ferch Madog, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1211. Nel 1170, all'età di 26 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come baron of cedewen nel Galles, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1244, nel Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 100 anni, e fu sepolto a Cardiganshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewainが約1145年にウェールズで生まれた時，父Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewainは37歳，母Dyddgu ferch Madogは26歳であった。彼はEfa ferch Maredudd Fychanとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1211年に兵役登録をした。1170年，26歳の時，イギリスウェールズでの職業はbaron of cedewenと記録されている。彼は1244年にイギリスウェールズカーディガンシュアイアイストラド-フルルで100歳で死去し，ウェールズカーディガンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain(이)가 대략 1145년 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Dyddgu ferch Madog(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1211년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1170년, 26세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 웨일스에서 baron of cedewen이었다. 그는 1244년 영국 웨일스 카디건실르 이스트라드-플루르에서 100세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 카디건실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain nasceu aproximadamente 1145, em Gales, seu pai, Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Dyddgu ferch Madog, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1211. Em 1170, com 26 anos, sua profissão aparece como baron of cedewen em Gales, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1244, em Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Gales, United Kingdom, com 100 anos, e foi sepultado em Cardiganshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain родился около 1145 г. в местности Уэльс, его отцу по имени Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Dyddgu ferch Madog, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. В 1211 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1170 году, в возрасте 26 лет, работал по профессии baron of cedewen в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. Он умер в 1244 г. в местности истрад-флур, кардиганьшир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 100 лет и был похоронен в местности кардиганьшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maredudd ap Robert Lord of Cedewain nació aproximadamente 1145, en Gales, su papá, Lord Robert ap Llywarch of Cydewain, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Dyddgu ferch Madog, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Efa ferch Maredudd Fychan. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1211. En 1170, a la edad de 26 años, su ocupación registrada era baron of cedewen en Gales, Reino Unido. Murió en 1244, en Ystrad-Flur, Cardiganshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 100 años, y fue sepultado en Cardiganshire, Gales.
